headline: Sundar Pichai on AI's flip phone moment
subtitle: Exclusive Google CEO interview · OpenAI Codex upgrades · California worker protections
intro: Google's I/O announcements reveal the company's top-to-bottom AI strategy. The Rundown sat down with CEO Sundar Pichai to discuss how AI will reshape work for creators, engineers, and everyday users—and why today's technology will look primitive within three years.

bullets.text: Sundar Pichai discusses Google's AI push at I/O 2026
bullets.summary: Pichai explained how models like Omni empower creators while maintaining YouTube's human-first ethos. He emphasized that agents will become ubiquitous within three years, handling 24/7 tasks across devices, and that success will be measured by agentic coding rather than AI-written code alone.

bullets.text: OpenAI rolls out new Codex features including goal mode
bullets.summary: OpenAI introduced Appshots for attaching app windows, goal mode for multi-hour tasks, locked computer use for automated work, and advanced annotation for web changes. These features expand Codex's utility and context awareness for developers working with agentic assistants.

bullets.text: Generate agent-native CLI tools from any website
bullets.summary: Printing Press enables agents to generate command-line tools for hard-to-use APIs like Google Flights. Users can install the toolkit, instruct agents to inspect examples, and build custom CLI commands for accessing public data sources.

bullets.text: California moves to protect workers from AI disruption
bullets.summary: Governor Newsom signed an executive order directing state agencies to develop worker protection policies including severance standards and universal basic capital. California will launch a tracking dashboard within 90 days and explore WARN Act updates as over 70K tech jobs have vanished in 2026.

bullets.text: Anthropic paying SpaceX $1.25B monthly for compute access
bullets.summary: SpaceX's IPO prospectus revealed Anthropic's substantial compute deal spanning through 2029. The arrangement provides access to Colossus and Colossus II infrastructure for model training and operations.

bullets.text: Brett Adcock's Hark raises $700M for personal AI platform
bullets.summary: Hark, co-founded by Figure AI's Brett Adcock, secured $700M at a $6B valuation to build an integrated personal intelligence system with native models, software, and hardware components.

bullets.text: Andrej Karpathy joins Anthropic's pre-training team
bullets.summary: Karpathy is leading a new group under Nick Joseph focused on using Claude to accelerate pre-training research. This represents significant talent acquisition that could strengthen Anthropic's model development capabilities.

bullets.text: Google launches Gemini Omni Flash with video generation
bullets.summary: The new model family aims for any input/any output capability, with Omni Flash generating and editing videos. The technical distinction between Omni and Veo mirrors the gap between Imagen and Nano Banana.

bullets.text: OpenAI model reportedly solves famous math conjecture
bullets.summary: An OpenAI model solved a notable mathematics problem with the proof verified by external mathematicians, showcasing advanced reasoning capabilities in specialized domains.

bullets.text: OpenAI launches public image verification tool
bullets.summary: The verifier checks C2PA metadata and Google's SynthID to authenticate images from ChatGPT, the API, and Codex, addressing growing concerns about AI-generated content provenance.

bullets.text: Figma design agent launches with parallel direction generation
bullets.summary: The agent can generate multiple design directions in parallel, make bulk edits, respect design systems, and collaborate in shared files, streamlining design workflows.

bullets.text: OpenAI reasoning model disproved 80-year Erdős conjecture
bullets.summary: OpenAI's internal reasoning model found a counterexample to the Erdős unit distance conjecture from 1946, proving it false by discovering infinite point arrangements that create more unit-distance pairs than previously believed possible. The proof used algebraic number theory and was verified by external mathematicians including Princeton's Will Sawin.

bullets.text: OpenAI leads Q1 revenue race, Anthropic projects Q2 surge
bullets.summary: OpenAI reportedly generated $5.7B in Q1, nearly $1B ahead of Anthropic, while Anthropic is projected to double to $10.9B in Q2, showing the rapidly shifting dynamics in AI company valuations and growth trajectories.

bullets.text: Qwen 3.7 Max ran 35-hour autonomous GPU optimization task
bullets.summary: Alibaba's Qwen 3.7 Max agent model completed a 35-hour autonomous GPU kernel optimization run involving 1,158 tool calls and 432 tests, reportedly delivering a 10x speedup on Alibaba hardware and demonstrating capability for extended autonomous work sessions.

bullets.text: Codex adds /goal mode for multi-step long-running tasks
bullets.summary: Codex now includes /goal mode that lets users define persistent objectives with outcome, constraints, and tests for complex tasks like migrations, refactors, and audits. The feature helps agents maintain focus on what 'done' means across many steps.

bullets.text: Spotify reportedly signs AI remix deal with major rights holders
bullets.summary: Spotify has reportedly reached an agreement enabling AI-generated remixes and variations through its music licensing partnerships, expanding the platform's AI capabilities for music creation and personalization.

bullets.text: Week's strangest AI failures highlight real-world deployment gaps
bullets.summary: The newsletter featured various AI deployment mishaps this week, underscoring ongoing challenges in moving theoretical AI capabilities into production environments where edge cases and real-world constraints matter significantly.

main_story.title: Sundar Pichai on Google's AI transformation
main_story.body: Google CEO Sundar Pichai sat down for an exclusive interview at I/O 2026 to discuss the company's sweeping AI initiative. He outlined how Google is embedding AI across YouTube, consumer products, and developer tools. Pichai emphasized that models like Omni enable better creative expression while YouTube remains fundamentally creator-centric. For everyday users, the draw is seamless integration and AI agents that handle tasks 24/7 across devices. For engineers, the paradigm shifts from measuring code written by AI to measuring agentic coding that manages long-running, complex tasks.
main_story.why: This represents a fundamental organizational pivot: AI isn't a feature but infrastructure. The shift will require creators, developers, and users to rethink how they work with technology daily.
main_story.take: Pichai's framing of today's AI as "flip phone moment" technology underscores the pace of change ahead. Organizations that adapt native AI workflows now will have substantial competitive advantages as the technology matures over the next three years.

around_the_horn.text: Anthropic in talks to use Microsoft's Maia chips alongside TPUs and Trainium
Manus exploring $1B raise to unwind Meta's $2B+ acquisition per China order
Microsoft and EY launch $1B partnership pairing engineers with 400K consultants
